componentDescription: SearchInput is a text input pre-configured for search interactions — includes a search icon, clear button, and optional search submission. Use it for filtering lists, searching databases, and any search UX pattern. For general text fields, use TextInput.
features: Built-in search icon on the left
Clear button that appears when input has value

usageExamples.title: Live filter search
usageExamples.description: Search that filters a list in real-time as the user types.
usageExamples.code:
<SearchInput
  placeholder="Search users..."
  value={searchQuery}
  onChange={setSearchQuery}
  onClear={() => setSearchQuery('')}
  size={TextInputSize.MEDIUM}
/>

usageExamples.title: Search with submit
usageExamples.description: Search input that triggers a search on Enter or button click.
usageExamples.code:
<SearchInput
  placeholder="Search documentation..."
  value={searchQuery}
  onChange={setSearchQuery}
  onSearch={handleSearch}
  onClear={() => { setSearchQuery(''); setResults([]); }}
  size={TextInputSize.LARGE}
/>

value.llmContext: Required. Controlled search query string. Manage with useState. For live-filter search, also use this value to filter your data — no separate state needed.

onChange.llmContext: Required. Called with new string on every keystroke. For live filtering, use this to filter your data directly. For submitted search, just update state and trigger search in onSearch.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with new value string on every keypress

onSearch.llmContext: Callback triggered when the user presses Enter. Use for server-side search that should only fire on explicit submission, not on every keystroke. If not provided, search only occurs via onChange.
onSearch.propDescription: Called when user presses Enter to submit search

onClear.llmContext: Callback fired when the user clicks the X clear button. Always set the search value to empty string here and clear any search results or reset filters.
onClear.propDescription: Called when user clicks the clear button

placeholder.llmContext: Hint text describing what can be searched. Use specific descriptions like 'Search users by name or email' rather than generic 'Search...' to help users understand the search scope.
placeholder.propDescription: Placeholder text in the search input
